meaning: n.濂堣壇(鏃ユ湰鏈窛宀涗腑瑗块儴鍩庡競)
examples: Nara houses one of the largest bronze Buddha statues in the world.
The world's biggest wooden building is the grand temple of Todai-ji in the city of Nara.
If you have some extra time, you might take a side trip to Nara, which was the capital before Kyoto.
In Japan the government is using its fibers in experimentally wiring an entire Japanese town, Nara.
"To date we have done nothing to stop the hemorrhaging of data at NARA, " he says.
